content.claim: A documented case in Encs shows that permission for a school to add lower grades (7-8) to an upper-secondary general school resulted in creaming of higher-status pupils from the final years of two primary schools, demonstrating that 6/8-year track expansion reshapes composition in feeder primary-school systems.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Single case study; generalizability to other municipalities not established
Causal direction not experimentally isolated; alternative explanations (residential migration, church affiliation) not ruled out
Long-term effects on primary-school students not measured
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
